name: Salto Picapedreros – circular by El Leoncillo - Montes de Málaga Natural Park
name_original: Salto Picapedreros – circular por El Leoncillo - Parque Natural Montes de Málaga
region_intro: Hiking around La Axarquía offers diverse landscapes within Andalusia, Spain, characterized by dramatic hills and mountains that form part of the Sierras de Alhama, Tejeda, and Almijara Natural Park. The region features varied topography, including rivers like the Río Chillar and El Saltillo gorge, lush oak and pine forests, extensive terraced orchards, and coastal sections along the Mediterranean Sea. Peaks in the area can reach over 1,500 meters, providing extensive views. Best hiking trails around La Axarquía The most popular hiking route is Rio Chillar Trail, an 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This route is known for its river walk, gorges, waterfalls, and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero El Saltillo from Canillas del Aceituno, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.0 km) path. This trail is renowned for its recently renovated suspension bridge and vistas. Local hikers also love the Cahorros and Felix Cross Route – circular from Frigiliana through the Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Mountain Range Natural Park, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through the Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Mountain Range Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around La Axarquía is defined by dramatic mountain ranges, river gorges, and coastal paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The routes in La Axarquía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8600 reviews. More than 50000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Axarquía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in La Axarquía, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: La Axarquía offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities with over 1100 trails. Approximately 515 of these are considered easy, 498 are moderate, and 146 are challenging, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in La Axarquía?
A: The region is rich in diverse natural features. You can explore dramatic hills and mountains within the Sierras de Alhama, Tejeda, and Almijara Natural Park, including peaks over 1,500 meters. Expect to encounter rivers and gorges like the &lt;strong&gt;Río Chillar&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Río Higuerón&lt;/strong&gt;, lush oak and pine forests, extensive terraced orchards, and even sections of coastal scenery along the Mediterranean. The area also features unique geological formations and stunning viewpoints.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in La Axarquía?
A: Yes, La Axarquía has many easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995575017/caves-of-nerja-eagle-aqueduct-maro-gorge-cove" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Nerja - Eagle Aqueduct - Maro Gorge Cove&lt;/a&gt; route offers a gentle 5.8 km path with historical and natural points of interest. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995557178/ciudad-jardin-puerto-de-la-torre-trail-pr-a-114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad Jardín - Puerto de la Torre Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 5 km and has minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls or river walks in the region?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Río Chillar&lt;/strong&gt; near Nerja is famous for its river walk, featuring gorges, waterfalls, and natural pools, making it a unique and refreshing experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995579249/rio-chillar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Chillar Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option for this. Additionally, the &lt;strong&gt;El Saltillo gorge&lt;/strong&gt; in Canillas de Aceituno offers thrilling views and a newly remodeled trail with a suspension bridge, accessible via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995954993/sendero-el-saltillo-from-canillas-del-aceituno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero El Saltillo from Canillas del Aceituno&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Axarquía?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995581742/cahorros-and-felix-cross-route-circular-from-frigiliana-through-the-tejeda-almijara-and-alhama-mountain-range-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros and Felix Cross Route&lt;/a&gt; from Frigiliana is a challenging circular trail through the natural park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995588500/collado-de-huerta-grande-circular-from-competa-parque-natural-sierra-de-tejeda-almijara-y-alhama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Huerta Grande – circular from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning views within the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Axarquía?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 8600 reviews. Reviewers often praise the dramatic mountain ranges, the unique river gorges, and the scenic coastal paths. The diversity of trails, from easy strolls to challenging ascents, is also frequently highlighted, along with the stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Hikers can discover various points of interest. Near Canillas de Aceituno, you can experience the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features several caves, such as the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/325807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja Cave&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in natural landscapes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/376453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enginal Pass&lt;/a&gt; in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park offers breathtaking views.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, La Axarquía offers several demanding routes. The Ascent to La Maroma, the highest peak in Malaga province, is a popular and challenging climb from Alcaucín, rewarding hikers with extensive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995581742/cahorros-and-felix-cross-route-circular-from-frigiliana-through-the-tejeda-almijara-and-alhama-mountain-range-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros and Felix Cross Route&lt;/a&gt; from Frigiliana is another difficult option, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that offer historical or cultural insights?
A: Many trails in La Axarquía weave through picturesque 'white villages' and past historical sites. The &lt;strong&gt;Caminito del Rey de la Axarquía&lt;/strong&gt;, connecting Cómpeta and Canillas de Aceituno, is a 24-kilometer linear route that passes Roman and Moorish bridges and charming villages like Salares and Sedella. The &lt;strong&gt;Ruta de los Molinos&lt;/strong&gt; between Canillas De Albaida and Árchez highlights old flour mills, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Axarquía?
A: The region is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer hikes. The &lt;strong&gt;Río Chillar&lt;/strong&gt; river walk is particularly refreshing from March to October. During the hotter summer months, early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, especially on shaded trails or those involving water.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic scenery?
A: Many trails in La Axarquía offer spectacular panoramic views due to the region's mountainous terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995584655/el-fuerte-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fuerte Trail&lt;/a&gt; from Frigiliana provides 360-degree views from El Fuerte Peak. The ascent to La Maroma also offers extensive vistas of the Natural Park, Sierra Nevada, and the Viñuela reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails accessible by public transport?
A: While many trailheads are best reached by car, some routes, especially those starting from larger towns like Nerja or Frigiliana, may have public transport connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific villages or trail starting points when planning your hike.
tips: Not the highest peak but has a nice view of the city. One can see the sunrise about 40min - 1h after the actual sunrise (as the mountains to the east are higher).

A very nice waterfall at the end of the river bed. There will be no water coming down in the summer months but at the end of the winter chances are that it has at least a litte bit of water coming down.
